stem: For the three-qubit operator `Pauli('XYZ')`, which qubit does the Z factor act on?
code:
from qiskit.quantum_info import Pauli

p = Pauli('XYZ')
print(p.to_matrix().shape)

options.text: Qubit 2 — the leftmost label character maps to the lowest-index qubit
Qubit 1 — Z is the middle factor
All three qubits — Z is applied globally
Qubit 0 — the rightmost label character maps to the lowest-index qubit

explanation.correct: Qiskit reads Pauli labels right-to-left. In 'XYZ' the rightmost character (Z) is qubit 0, Y is qubit 1, and X is qubit 2. So Z acts on qubit 0.

distractors.A: Reverses the little-endian convention. The leftmost character (X) is the highest-index qubit (qubit 2), so Z — the rightmost — is qubit 0, not qubit 2.
distractors.B: Y is the middle factor (qubit 1); Z is the rightmost factor and acts on qubit 0.
distractors.C: Each character is a separate tensor factor acting on one qubit; Z acts on a single qubit, not all three.
